title: Debug a Service with No Endpoints
description:
A Service exists but curl returns 'Connection refused'. Diagnose why the Service has no Endpoints and fix the root cause — a selector mismatch between the Service and the Deployment's Pod labels.

**Why this matters**: Selector mismatch is the single most common Service bug. Learning to spot empty Endpoints instantly narrows down 80% of Service connectivity issues.

**Common Pitfalls**: The fix must be in the Service selector, not the Deployment labels (changing labels would break other things). Always check Endpoints before assuming a network policy or firewall is to blame.
steps: Apply the broken manifests with kubectl apply -f solution.yaml
Check endpoints: kubectl get endpoints web-backend-svc — notice the list is empty
Inspect the Deployment labels: kubectl get deployment web-backend -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels}'
Inspect the Service selector: kubectl get svc web-backend-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector}'
Fix the Service selector to match the actual Deployment label (app=web-backend)
Reapply and verify endpoints are now populated, then test connectivity
hints: Run 'kubectl get endpoints web-backend-svc' — empty subsets means no Pods matched the selector
Compare the Service's spec.selector with the Deployment's spec.template.metadata.labels carefully
The Deployment labels use app=web-backend but the Service selector uses app=backend — spot the difference
After fixing and reapplying, endpoints should show Pod IPs within a few seconds

editor_config.initial_code:
# --- Deployment: labels are app=web-backend ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-backend
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web-backend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web-backend   # <-- actual Pod label
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web-backend
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- Service: selector is WRONG (app=backend instead of app=web-backend) ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: web-backend-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: backend           # TODO: fix this — it does not match the Deployment label
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# --- Curl client to test connectivity ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: debug-client
spec:
  containers:
  - name: curl
    image: curlimages/curl:latest
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]

_solution.code_solution:
# --- Deployment: labels are app=web-backend ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-backend
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web-backend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web-backend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web-backend
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- Service: selector FIXED to match Deployment label ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: web-backend-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: web-backend       # fixed: matches Deployment Pod template label
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# --- Curl client to test connectivity ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: debug-client
spec:
  containers:
  - name: curl
    image: curlimages/curl:latest
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

echo "Waiting for web-backend Deployment to be ready..."
kubectl rollout status deployment/web-backend --timeout=90s
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "✗ web-backend Deployment did not become ready"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ web-backend Deployment is ready"

echo "Waiting for debug-client Pod..."
timeout=60
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  phase=$(kubectl get pod debug-client -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null || echo "Pending")
  if [ "$phase" = "Running" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done
if [ "$phase" != "Running" ]; then
  echo "✗ debug-client Pod did not reach Running state"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ debug-client Pod is running"

SVC_SELECTOR=$(kubectl get svc web-backend-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector.app}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$SVC_SELECTOR" != "web-backend" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service selector app='$SVC_SELECTOR', expected 'web-backend'"
  echo "  The selector mismatch is still present — fix spec.selector.app to match the Deployment label"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service selector is correct (app=web-backend)"

ENDPOINT_COUNT=$(kubectl get endpoints web-backend-svc -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses}' 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; data=sys.stdin.read(); print(len(json.loads(data)) if data.strip() and data.strip()!='null' else 0)" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$ENDPOINT_COUNT" -lt 1 ] 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "✗ Service web-backend-svc still has no endpoints"
  kubectl get endpoints web-backend-svc
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service web-backend-svc has $ENDPOINT_COUNT endpoint(s)"

HTTP_CODE=$(kubectl exec debug-client -- curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://web-backend-svc 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
  echo "✓ Connectivity verified: HTTP $HTTP_CODE from debug-client to web-backend-svc"
  exit 0
else
  echo "✗ HTTP request returned $HTTP_CODE (expected 200)"
  exit 1
fi
